author: @TrollFaceTheMan
text: A capacitor used to smooth the output to the board would potentially be a good idea too. Just to help prevent fluctuations potentially causing voltage spikes as the board turns on and off repeatedly.

author: @TrollFaceTheMan
text:
Unfortunately the only problem with a full bridge rectifier there is a over 1v drop across two diodes losing potentially quite a bit of power.

 I have been thinking of this a lot lately and planning to make a generator out of a drill I have too keeping the gear box intact but overriding an external turn lock they have. 

 The gear box is nice because I can set the speed I want to turn the drill at to get voltage much like gears on a bike.

 Video is inspired by yours so thank you.

author: @Kiyarose3999
text: Nice one for adapting to ph charger, that is from a 12v Gene, I was wondering if the 240v Synchronous motor in Microwave cookers could do the same, if connected to a 240v plug ph charger. The reason I ask is cos those motors only need a slow turn to generate 240v, so avoiding the need for much faster rpm with the 10v DC Hand Drill motor you used. I have an old plastic face cream tub that I found, that has a good screw lid, that I plan on putting a microwave motor in it and the circuit board from a 240v plug ph charger, the crank handle also fits in the tub. So if the wiring works out, I will have a compact pocket ph etc charger, that will have a USB socket  in the side of the tub. So just unscrew the lid to get the crank handle, the motor bolted to the inside of the lid, with the spindle going through the lid. Also could be a hand cranked portable light if USB wired to LED’s, like you did.

author: @johnoddvar1967
text: Hope you se me of one that know a litle how to do it. :) But of the drilbit from a nail i am totaly amased (I have to test that next week).  Though I think you should mention that if you like to charge a phone from 5 volts you need to shorcut the datawires. You can pick up 5 volts from a usb plug, but the phone need the lines shortcuted. F.eks if you cut of chargeing cable and put it to your powersuply. I used to put extra windings on a transformer and rectify it (Of course i had experimented la litle on my own in privat). And shoown my aprendices hov to of course charge your phone. I played a litle tuff so i just cut the chargeing cable and explaned how. I used a serial coil and ar capacitor though. Its for showing that if you put 10 turns it wil show the voltage pr turn nearly close and you can sort ofut the turns in the rest of the transformer. Then you just have to count out the number of turns that multipyed with the sqere root of 2 gives about 5 volt. of course you have to remove 1,2 volt for the rectifyer. (I think its already done the shortcut in your aplication)

author: @matthewellisor5835
text:
I do like those categories of commenters!

I'd say that I know enough to get by about a few things and next to nothing about almost everything.

Yet many people express amazement at some of the things that I do. I try to tell them that I DON'T actually know what I'm doing but I want to learn so I'll search out what information and teaching I can find then give it a go.

Too many are, I think, afraid of failure. Let's keep encouraging them to "give it a go" while seeking out the information available.

You shouldn't praise failure but just because my project didn't function, if my goal was to learn, it was a success! I learned, at least and probably much more, that this way doesn't work.

replies.text: you and I seem to think very alike mate - cheers

author: @ThomasAndersonbsf
text: you or luke should show people how to put a capacitor on that output when using it on the light, so it stops flickering and can even possibly be used to give it longer lasting light between cranks, (ie crank for a few seconds and get a minute or even half a minute of lasting light, use more and you can crank for a full minute and get 4 or 5 minutes of run time off it quickly and not worry about charge cycles and such like you do with batteries and such, (and you have shown people how to make capacitors before, so it should tie in nicely to those videos!)

replies.author: @ThomasAndersonbsf
replies.text: ok I just saw the end of the video and saw you mentioned the cap idea LOL, would be nice to see some info on input output and stability and all maybe with diodes so the power only goes into the cap, from the charger, and not back through the coils in the generator, and out only to the LED light since it is polar (a diode and thus the power will not work pushing it the wrong way, though it will go with enough voltage and damage the LED so yeah something to warn about getting it the right way around and how to check :) (usually the power is far more than you are using to light it and you are good but there is that chance when lighting up one tiny little LED for one reason or another)

replies.author: @ThomasAndersonbsf
replies.text:
oh also putting in lights both directions is a good idea to lower the flicker by doubling the light output frequency, till it is like a CFL lamp, which flicker too, but too fast to see, 

jusst dont do this from the back seat of a friends car at night with a red one way and blue the other, (police colors) cause, as I found out, the look like approaching police that typically turn lights on to arrest someone, and they (the friend) pulled over before realizing it was me, (and it was actually old white LED and red which the white had too much blue tint, thus fooling them) so yeah they were quite mad initially and took weeks before they could laugh about that due to how bad it scared them LOL

author: @headlessxp4948
text: If you wanted, you could increase the efficiency of the whole thing by removing the buck converter in the middle since the car usb charger thingy is basically another buck converter. So your just wasting (hard earned) power by chaining the two converters. Or keep your buck converter and instead of hooking up the car usb charger just solder on a USB A socket.

replies.author: @shanealexander9952
replies.text: I was thinking the same thing.  One thing to note:  I have a 12v drill with working batteries.  If I go to charge the battery using the crank It will get to the point where I let go of the crank and the battery will turn the drill.  I had to put a couple of 12v diodes in  place between the battery and drill.  (inside an automotive fuse addon wire).  You can bypass this if you want to use the drill as a drill or just direct charging by switching out the Diode fuses with a real fuse or a bypass switch.  My origional drill is intact, I just rubber band or tape the trigger to hand crank it. There is probably a better way but I haven't worked on it much.  PS I can't get the batteries fully charged this way the torque gets to hard.

replies.author: @nil5221
replies.text: I agree, I thought the same thing. The car USB charger will handle up to 48v  so the regulator is superfluous in that particular application.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: the data sheet on the car usb charger said that it accepts a 12-24v input mate the hand crank generator varies from 2 - 10v - my thought was the the first boosted voltage to a level the car USB port could use

replies.author: @nil5221
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering could you say what the first voltage regulator is? I thought it  looked like one of the XL4015 series  which are buck, not boost, and the initial no-load cranking was up somewhere around 30v (I thought you said during your mad laughing ) so I assumed you had used one of those.

replies.author: @headlessxp4948
replies.text: @nil5221 Looks very much like a XL4015 buck indeed.

author: @ravenrock541
text:
A thought challenge (but off topic): I am sure you are familiar with a Ram Pump. Could a Ram pump be designed to generate electricity? I'm thinking of a Cyl wrapped in copper with a magnet in the center that is forced upwards by the Ram pump pulse. Electrical current would be generated on both the upward and downward motion. To get a modest range of motion I think a large check valve (for volume) would be needed, then the pulse condensed to a small volume (to increase pressure). Just thinking out loud so don't mind me, but a Ram pump never stops running if there is water flow. So,  this would provide constant electrical charge for a battery or some such.
   Oh, and thank you for taking the time to read this. Your vids always interest me.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it certainly could mate and I like your design - it makes sense to e - I personally love ram pumps too!

author: @ArcanusLibero
text: Has anyone tried to put a flywheel on their's to stabilize the flow of electricity? It might be really cool if the hand crank could be clutched so you don't lose control when you can't keep up.

replies.author: @ghostmandeak7305
replies.text: What's that mate

replies.author: @ArcanusLibero
replies.text: @ghostmandeak7305 I was thinking that if you put a flywheel the weight of it would help you to stabilize or balance the turn rate of the motor adding to the consistency of the power out. It was a problem we had when we were kids and took the batteries out of our vespas and ran the electronics off the stator. Then if you clutch the crank handle it won't spin when you let go or can't keep up with the turn rate of the flywheel. This in theory might make the power signal a little cleaner and work a little more efficiently with delicate electronics. Just an Idea. I was wondering if anyone else had tried any or part of the idea on their own already. No point in making mistakes other's have overcome.

replies.author: @ghostmandeak7305
replies.text: @ArcanusLibero mate I haven't a clue pal me I'm useless unless I'm shown something but I'm looking for ideas how to charge a project of mine involving a recumbent trike I'm building with a micro caravan on the back or camper even I am looking at solar wind an trying to find ways to drag power from wheel power also mate as I'm looking at travelling all over the UK for a YouTube project I have planned and obviously I need the trike to be powered electronically but when the trailers on I will have two motors attached so I will have two sets of batteries for each motor and I will have an electric generator for emergencies mate as I dont want to end up stranded

replies.author: @ArcanusLibero
replies.text: @ghostmandeak7305 I would recommend a propane generator for back up power. They make them very small and they can run on camp can propane. Usually 4-6 hours on a 2lb can. Fly wheels are usually heavy so I am not sure there is an applications that would suit your needs. I do think there were some projects on this channel that included modified Flywheels that may interest you. They were innovative in the way they were constructed.  A small flywheel or dynamo could be useful for a hand crank generator like the one made in this video but it is not essential if you have a battery system as a buffer to clean up your sine wave. Sadly no easy or guaranteed fixes just ideas. Maybe someone else that watches these videos has more experience. Good luck....

replies.author: @ghostmandeak7305
replies.text: @ArcanusLibero thank you very much pal you've been a great help buddy and I will research that thanks I think solar and wind and leave the wheels alone unless I can find a really light none resistance way of doing this then j will leave it for now but thanks alot pal stay safe and take care yeah all the best bye
